---
title: ""
page-layout: full
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

<br><br>

At Cambridge, I teach a class on "Digital capitalism" for the [MPhil in
Development
Studies](https://www.postgraduate.study.cam.ac.uk/courses/directory/podvmpmdv).

In the past, I have taught or designed the following courses:

**Ecole Normale Supérieure, Paris**

-   Economic and urban geography \[graduate level\]

-   Mapping and spatial analysis with R \[graduate level, Master in
    Quantitative Social Sciences\]

-   Statistics and mapping with R \[undergraduate level\]

-   Introduction to urban fieldwork \[undergraduate and graduate level\]

-   Masterclass "[Digital Technologies in the African
    city](https://jmigozzi.github.io/masterclass/)" \[graduate level\]

    *A 10-day graduate masterclass in partnership with the Ecole Normale
    Supérieure and the African Center for Cities, with participants from
    Nigeria, France and South Africa. The program featured guest
    speakers, collective workshops, site visits, and participant-led
    research projects.*

-   Masterclass "Researching urban public spaces" \[graduate level\]

    *A 10-day graduate masterclass organized by the Ecole Normale
    Supérieure (France), the Universities of Lagos (Nigeria) and the
    French Institute for Research in Africa (Nigeria). The program
    featured guest speakers, collective workshops, site visits, and
    participant-led research projects.*

**University Grenoble Alpes**

-   Urban geographies of globalisation \[undergraduate level\]

-   Mainstream policies and grassroot initiatives for local development
    \[graduate level\]

-   Geographic Information Systems \[undergraduate level\]
